[[Category:Famous gemstones]] [[Category:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Jewelry]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasakani no Magatama}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

revision.text:
'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

revision.text:
'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}}

In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

revision.text:
'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

[[Category:Famous gemstones]] [[Category:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Jewelry]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yasakani no Magatama}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yasakani no Magatama-qq]] [[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

[[Category:Famous gemstones]] [[Category:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Jewelry]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yasakani no Magatama}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yasakani no Magatama—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

== See Also == * {{ill|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|ja|三種の神器}} * {{ill|Regalia|ja|レガリア}}
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

[[Category:Famous gemstones]] [[Category:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Jewelry]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yasakani no Magatama}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Yasakani no Magatama-->[[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

'''八尺瓊勾玉'''（やさかにのまがたま）は、[[八咫鏡]]・[[天叢雲剣]]と共に[[日本神話]]の[[三種の神器]]（みくさのかむだから・さんしゅのじんぎ）の1つ。'''八坂瓊曲玉'''とも書く。
== 形態 == 大きな[[勾玉]]とも、長い緒に繋いだ勾玉ともされる。
=== 名称からの推察 === [[ファイル:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|瑪瑙の勾玉（参考画像）]] 「さか」は通常は「しゃく」（[[尺]]）の転訛だが<ref>『[[広辞苑]]』など各種辞典</ref>、この場合は上代の長さの単位の[[咫]]（あた）のことである{{要出典|date=2012年10月}}。8尺は（当時の尺は今より短いため）約180センチメートル (cm)、8咫は約140cmである。
この長さは、玉の周とも、尾を含めた長さであるとも、結わえてある緒の長さであるとも言う。また、「八尺」は単に大きい（あるいは長い）という意味であるとも、「弥栄」（いやさか）が転じたものとする説もある。
「瓊（に）」の語意は美しい玉、特に赤い美玉のことともされ、そこからこれは[[メノウ|瑪瑙]]（メノウ）のことであるともされる（現代の[[若狭めのう細工|瑪瑙細工]]では深紅の赤瑪瑙が細工物や勾玉などによく使用され、ありふれた色だが、これは江戸時代に原石を加熱して赤く発色させる技法が発明されてよりの事である）。
『[[風土記]]』のうち『越後国風土記』の逸文では、「八坂丹（やさかに）は玉の名なり。謂ふ、玉の色青し。故、'''青八坂丹の玉'''と云う也」と記されていることから、青緑色の[[ヒスイ製勾玉]]であったか、そう認識されていたことがわかる。[[古墳時代]]の発掘品や、中国の史書にも書かれたように、ヒスイ製勾玉が珍重されたことは明らかである。
== 位置づけ == 璽<ref>璽は、本来は「高貴な人が持つ印」の意味である（[[wikt:璽]]）。日本でも天皇の印は御璽・国璽という（[[:en:wikt:璽]]／[https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 コトバンク 璽]）。</ref> と呼ぶこともあり、やはり三種の神器のひとつである剣とあわせて「[[剣璽]]」と称される。
『[[養老令]]』の[[神祇令]]に {{Quotation|およそ[[践祚]]の日、[[忌部氏|忌部]]、神璽の鏡剣（かがみたち）を上（たてまつ）れ}} との記述があり、事実『[[日本書紀]]』には、[[690年]]（持統天皇4年）の[[持統天皇]]即位を初めとして、忌部氏が「神璽の剣鏡」を奉ったとある。ここで玉に関する言及がないのだが、これについては以下のような諸説<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[日本大百科全書]] | version=[[コトバンク]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[小学館]] | author=[[直木孝次郎]] }}</ref> がある。 * 「三種の神器」として問題ないとする諸説 ** 玉も神器の1つだったが、身に着ける宝であり、献上される品ではなかった ** 漢文特有の表現上の問題であって実際には鏡剣玉の3つをさしている *** 「鏡剣玉」を略して2字で代表させている *** 「神璽」が玉のことをさしている（『日本書紀』の原文では「神璽剣鏡」であり「神璽・剣・鏡」と3つに読むことが可能である） *** 「神璽」が神器全体の意と、鏡剣に対して玉をさす意を兼ねている * 鏡剣と玉との間に落差や経緯の違いを想定する諸説 ** 玉は神器としての重要性が劣り、宝としては鏡剣より軽いと考えられていた ** 本来もともと3種であり[[天智天皇|天智]]朝に定められた即位儀礼までは3種であったがなぜか『[[飛鳥浄御原令]]』で鏡剣の2種に改められその後またすぐ3種に戻った * 三種の神器と称するのは後世の創作された物語の上でのことにすぎず、神器の真実は鏡剣の「二種の神器」だったとする説
元々は二種の神器であり勾玉は含まれていなかったという説については、807年頃に[[斎部広成]]が著した歴史書『[[古語拾遺]]』の「…八腿鏡及草薙剣の二種の神宝を以て,皇孫に授け賜ひて,永に天璽〔所謂神璽の剣・鏡是なり。〕と為たまふ。…（前後略）」という記述などが根拠とされている。
勾玉の性質として、「日（陽）」を表す八咫鏡に対して「月（陰）」を表しているのではないかという説がある。 仮にそうであるなら、[[八咫鏡]]=[[天照大神]]、[[天叢雲剣]]=[[須佐之男命]]、八尺瓊勾玉=[[月読尊]]で、[[三種の神器]]は[[三貴子]]を象徴していると見る事もできる。
== 所在 == === 経緯 === [[奈良時代]]には後宮の[[蔵司]]が保管したが、[[平安時代]]ころからは、剣と共に櫃に入れて天皇の身辺に置かれた<ref name="kotobank"/>。
[[冷泉天皇]]は、精神病あるいは発達障害のため奇行が多かったが、勾玉の箱をあけて実物を確認しようとしたこともあった。しかし箱を封じている紐を解くと白い煙が湧き出てきたため、恐れおののき実物の確認を中断した<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|和書|author=竹田恒泰|authorlink=竹田恒泰|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=「皇室のきょうかしょ」vol.22 神器各論?八坂瓊曲玉（やさかにのまがたま）|publisher=フジテレビ|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100828123157/https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|archivedate=2010-08-28|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>。また大江匡房の談話録によれば、夜間、側近が宮中からの急用と聞いて駆けつけ、女房に天皇の居場所を問うと、冷泉天皇は清涼殿の寝所におられて、たった今、安置してある御璽を包む紐を解いて開くよう言われたと答えた。驚いて天皇の部屋に押し入ると、本当に箱の紐を解いているところだったため、それを奪い取って元通りに結び直したという<ref>『江談抄 [{{NDLDC|3441719/173}} 冷泉院御璽の結緒を解き開かんと欲し給ふ事]』 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション</ref>。
平安時代末期の[[寿永]]4年3月24日（[[1185年]]4月25日）、[[壇ノ浦の戦い]]で[[平時子|二位尼]]が[[安徳天皇]]を抱き入水したとき、玉・剣と共に（『[[平家物語]]』によると「神璽を脇に挟み宝剣を腰に差し」）沈んだ。しかし玉は箱に入っていたため、箱ごと浮かび上がり、源氏に回収された。あるいは、一度失われたものの、[[源頼朝]]の命を受けた漁師の岩松与三が、網で鏡と玉を引き揚げたとも言う。
[[室町時代]]の[[嘉吉]]3年9月23日（[[1443年]]10月16日）に起こった[[禁闕の変]]の際に、[[後南朝]]勢力によって宝剣とともに宮中から奪われ、宝剣は翌日発見されたが神璽は[[大和国]]奥吉野へ持ち去られ、その後約15年間、後南朝勢力が保有した。[[長禄]]元年（[[1457年]]）12月に[[赤松氏]]の遺臣らが奥吉野の後南朝の行宮を襲い、南朝の皇胤である[[自天王]]と[[忠義王]]の兄弟を討って、神璽を持ち去ろうとしたが失敗、翌長禄2年（[[1458年]]）3月末、赤松遺臣らは自天王の母の屋敷を襲い、神璽を奪い去る事に成功した（[[長禄の変]]）。その後、神璽は大和国[[越智氏 (大和国)|越智氏]]の在所に移された後、同年8月30日、宮中に戻された。
=== 現状 === [[1989年]]（[[昭和]]64年）[[1月7日]]に第125代天皇[[明仁]]が[[践祚]]した後、明仁の神器として[[皇居]]にある[[御所]]の「[[剣璽]]の間」に、剣（[[形代]]）とともに保管されていたが<ref name="kyokasyo22" />、[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）[[5月1日]]の第126代天皇[[徳仁]]の践祚に伴い[[東宮御所|赤坂御所]]で剣（形代）とともに保管されることになった<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |date=2019-05-01 |title=新天皇陛下「国民に寄り添い、象徴の責務果たす」 |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[日本経済新聞社]] |newspaper=[[日本経済新聞]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>。[[2021年]]（令和3年）[[9月6日]]、今上天皇一家が赤坂御所から皇居に移転し、再び御所の「剣璽の間」に剣（形代）とともに納められた<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |date=2021-09-06 |title=天皇ご一家、皇居へご転居|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20210906-DBW7BYPZRJNS3EAQBJB5KYWWVY/|publisher=[[産経新聞社]] |newspaper=[[産経新聞]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |和書 |date=2021-09-23 |title=天皇家の引っ越し 三種の神器「勾玉」を28年前に運んだ元侍従が明かす秘話「天皇は日本文化の継承者」|url=https://dot.asahi.com/articles/-/66300?page=1|publisher=[[朝日新聞出版]] |newspaper=[[AERA dot.]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>。
[[剣璽#剣璽動座|剣璽動座]]に備え[[トヨタ・センチュリーロイヤル]]の後部座席には、八尺瓊勾玉と天叢雲剣を安置するための台座が設置できるようになっている。
== 神話での記述 == [[日本神話]]では、[[岩戸隠れ]]の際に後に玉造連の祖神となる[[玉祖命]]が作り、八咫鏡とともに[[フトダマ|太玉命]]が捧げ持つ[[サカキ|榊]]の木に掛けられた。後に[[天孫降臨]]に際して[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]に授けられたとするが、[[神武東征]]では言及されていない。
『[[古事記]]』には、八尺瓊勾玉（緒に通した勾玉）の後ろに、さらに『五百津之美須麻流之珠』（やさかにのまがたまのいほつのみすまるのたま）という、数の多さを形容した語が付く。
尚、『[[日本書紀]]』神代で八尺瓊曲玉が言及される別の部分として、六段{{ruby|一書|あるふみ}}第二で羽明玉という神が[[素戔嗚尊]]に、スサノヲが[[天照大神]]に会う（[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]）前に「瑞八坂瓊之曲玉」を渡している。
『日本書紀』は[[垂仁天皇]]87年条で、昔[[丹波国]]の[[桑田郡|桑田村]]の[[甕襲]]の家にいた足往（あゆき）という犬が牟士那（むじな）という獣を食い殺し、その獣の腹にあった八尺瓊の勾玉が献上され、この玉が今[[石上神宮]]にあると記している<ref>[[森浩一]]は『日本神話の考古学』で、この言い伝えは草薙剣がヤマタノオロチの体内にあったという言い伝えと共通しており、この玉が後に皇室の勾玉になった可能性があると考えを述べている。</ref>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:やさかにのまかたま}} [[Category:著名な宝石]] [[Category:三種の神器]] [[Category:ビーズ細工]]

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}} and the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja|天叢雲剣}}. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『{{ill|Kojien|ja|広辞苑}}』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that during the accession of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, it was kept in the court's treasury. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Reizei|ja|冷泉天皇}}, of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} (1185), it sank into the sea along with {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}, but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|皇居}}. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.

The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of {{ill|Agate|ja|メノウ}}, as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}, specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel.
