[[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]
<!--Yasakani no Magatama-->

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|qq=}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|qq=}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

[[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]
<!--Yasakani no Magatama--> [[Category:Beadwork]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Individual gemstones]]

'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|qq=}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|qq=}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

<!--Yasakani no Magatama--> [[Category:Beadwork]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Individual gemstones]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yasakani-no-Magatama}} [[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[it:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]] [[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|qq=}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}}

== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|qq=}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==

Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

[[sn:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]
<!--Yasakani no Magatama--> [[Category:Beadwork]]
[[Category:Individual gemstones]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yasakani-no-Magatama}} [[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[it:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]] [[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10892137}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|qq=|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==

There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

[[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]
<!--Yasakani no Magatama--> [[Category:Beadwork]]
[[Category:Individual gemstones]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yasakani-no-Magatama}} [[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[it:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]] [[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10892137}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[fa:یاساکانی نو ماگاتاما]][[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[it:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]][[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]][[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|qq=|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|qq=|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

[[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]]
<!--Yasakani no Magatama--> [[Category:Beadwork]]
[[Category:Individual gemstones]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yasakani-no-Magatama}} [[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[it:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]] [[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10892137}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[fa:یاساکانی نو ماگاتاما]][[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[it:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]][[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]][[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.

[[Category:Individual gemstones]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yasakani-no-Magatama}} [[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]] [[it:Yasakani no Magatama]] [[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]] [[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10892137}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has English label - based on Wikidata English label
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[fa:یاساکانی نو ماگاتاما]][[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[it:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]][[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]][[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[fa:یاساکانی نو ماگاتاما]][[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[it:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]][[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]][[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[fa:یاساکانی نو ماگاتاما]][[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[it:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]][[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]][[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]
== External Links == *{{Kotobank|三種の神器（宝物）}} *{{Kotobank|三種の神器}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[en:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[fa:یاساکانی نو ماگاتاما]][[fr:Yasakani-no-Magatama]][[it:Yasakani no Magatama]][[ja:八尺瓊勾玉]][[pl:Szczęścionośne krzywulce]][[zh:八尺琼勾玉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892137]] Wikidata name: Yasakani no Magatama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasakani no Magatama''' (八尺瓊勾玉) is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]] (Sanshu no Jingi) in [[Japanese mythology]], alongside the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Yata no Kagami]] and the [[:en:Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi]]. It is also written as 八坂瓊曲玉.
== Form == It is said to either be a large [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] or magatama strung together on a long cord.
=== Interpretation of the Name === [[File:Magatama40.JPG|thumb|Magatama made of agate (example image)]] The term "saka" is usually a corruption of "shaku" (尺, a unit of measurement)<ref>『[[:en:Kōjien|Kojien]]』and other dictionaries</ref>, but in this case, it may refer to the ancient unit of length [[Ata (unit)|咫]] (ata){{Citation needed|date=October 2012}}. Eight shaku is approximately 180 centimeters (cm) by ancient standards, while eight ata is about 140 cm.
This length may refer to the circumference of the jewel, its total length including its "tail," or the length of the cord attached to it. Additionally, "eight shaku" could simply imply great size or length. Another theory suggests that "yasaka" derives from "iyasaka" (弥栄), meaning "prosperity."
The word "ni" (瓊) refers to beautiful jewels, particularly red gems. It is thus considered to be made of [[:en:Agate|Agate]], as red agate was commonly used for magatama and other ornaments in modern {{ill|Wakasa agate crafts|ja|若狭めのう細工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa agate crafts|1=Wakasa agate crafts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616615}}. This red color often results from a heating process developed in the Edo period.
In the [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]], specifically an excerpt from the {{ill|Echigo Fudoki|ja|越後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is described as "Yasakani is the name of the jewel. It is said that the jewel's color is blue, hence the name 'Ao-Yasakani no Tama' (青八坂丹の玉)." This suggests it was likely a jade magatama or at least perceived as such. Jade magatama were highly valued, as evidenced by artifacts from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and references in Chinese historical texts.
== Symbolism == Also called a "jewel" (璽)<ref>璽 originally referred to "the seal of a noble person" ({{ill|Wiktionary|en|璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiktionary|qq=}}). In Japan, the emperor's seals are referred to as御璽 or国璽 ([https://kotobank.jp/word/璽-515296 Kotobank - 璽]).</ref>, it is associated with the other two treasures and referred to collectively as {{ill|Kenji|ja|剣璽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji|1=Sword and Jewel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397925}}.
The {{ill|Yoro Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}'s section on {{ill|Jingi|ja|神祇令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingi|qq=}} states: {{Quotation|On the day of [[Accession to the Chrysanthemum Throne|践祚]], the [[:en:Inbe clan|Imibe clan]], shall present the sacred mirror and sword (神璽の鏡剣).}} The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that during the accession of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] in [[:en:690|690]], the Imibe clan presented the "sacred mirror and sword." Although there is no explicit mention of the jewel, several theories attempt to address this omission<ref name="kotobank">{{cite | url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%89%E7%A8%AE%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E5%99%A8%28%E5%AE%9D%E7%89%A9%29-1540193#E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E5.85.A8.E6.9B.B8.28.E3.83.8B.E3.83.83.E3.83.9D.E3.83.8B.E3.82.AB.29 | title=[[Japan Encyclopedia]] | chapter=三種の神器（宝物） | publisher=[[Shogakukan]] | author=Kojirō Naoki }}</ref>: * The jewel was always part of the treasures but was considered personal adornment rather than an object to be presented. * The term "sacred mirror and sword" might be a shorthand for the complete set of three treasures.
== Location == === Historical Background === In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], it was kept in the court's treasury. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it, along with the sword, was stored in a chest near the emperor<ref name="kotobank"/>.
There are accounts, such as during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Reizei|Emperor Reizei]], of attempts to open the container housing the jewel, resulting in mysterious occurrences that deterred further examination<ref name="kyokasyo22">{{Cite web|author=Takehito Takeda|url=https://www.fujitv.co.jp/takeshi/takeshi/column/koshitsu/koshitsu22.html|title=皇室のきょうかしょ Vol.22 八尺瓊曲玉|publisher=Fujitv|accessdate=2019-05-06}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] (1185), it sank into the sea along with [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], but it was recovered, reportedly floating in its case.
=== Current Status === The Yasakani no Magatama is now housed in the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]]. It was last ceremonially moved during the {{ill|2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja|平成から令和への皇位継承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019 Japanese Imperial transition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-05-01 |title=New Emperor's Commitment |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO44379480R00C19A5MM0000/|publisher=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]] |accessdate=2024-04-21 }}</ref>.
== References ==
{{reflist|2}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] * [[:en:Regalia|Regalia]]
